hook.text: Two users update different rows at the same time. Row-level locks let both proceed without waiting.

buildup.text: When a transaction updates a row, it acquires a lock on that specific row. Other rows remain unlocked.

discovery.text: Another transaction can read the row (in MVCC databases) but can't update it until the lock is released.

twist.text: SELECT ... FOR UPDATE explicitly locks rows for reading. Use it when you plan to update based on what you read.

climax.text: Row-level locks are fine-grained. They maximize concurrency by only blocking the specific rows being modified.

punchline.text: Row locks: precise targeting. Only the affected rows are blocked.

quiz.question: When should you use SELECT ... FOR UPDATE?
options: When you read a row and plan to update it, to prevent other transactions from changing it first
When you want to read data faster
For every SELECT query to ensure data safety
